what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record that documents the `company` control CLI as a PATH symlink at `/home/tim/.local/bin/company`, explains that it resolves into the repo via a thin shim at `~/company/ops/company`, and describes its role as the single interactive control surface for the Company system.
observations: The PATH entry is a 29-byte symlink: `/home/tim/.local/bin/company -> /home/tim/company/ops/company`
The symlink target `ops/company` is a thin launcher shim that runs `cli/app.py` next to it
Relation is marked `external` because the PATH pointer is outside the repo, but the actual body is in-repo
It is invoked directly by the user/agents and has no systemd service unit
It is tagged `control` and listed as `part-of: [[company]]`
Status is `unconfirmed`, coverage shows 1/1 files read, and there are no secrets, models, ports, or service units
standouts: The only Company-specific artifact in `~/.local` is this single symlink
The CLI is described as the one control surface for the whole Company system
The symlink resolves back into the repo through an intermediate shim rather than directly to the Python file
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema with type, register, aliases, path, relation, kind, status, dates, coverage, and tags
Uses `relation: external` with an explicit note clarifying the in-repo body
Uses `part-of` to link the entry to the parent `[[company]]` entity
Includes an advisor note to disambiguate the `external` classification
concerns: Coverage is only 1/1 files, so the full CLI package has not been explored
Symlink indirection through a shim adds a fragile hop if the shim path or `cli/app.py` location changes
`last_active` equals `created`, suggesting little observed usage since creation
notes: A ledger record that treats the `company` PATH symlink as an external pointer whose real implementation lives inside `~/company/ops`.
questions: What are the exact contents and permissions of `ops/company` beyond its header?
How is the symlink installed or maintained?
What commands and subcommands does `cli/app.py` expose?
Why is the status `unconfirmed` if the symlink and shim were read?
Is there any documentation or shell completion for the CLI?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the Company CLI PATH entry and its in-repo resolution; the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the boundary between the external PATH alias and the in-repo CLI code explicit, so future readers do not treat the symlink as a separate or external implementation.
novelty: It models a symlink as a first-class terrain entry with a nuanced `external` relation that explicitly means 'external PATH pointer, in-repo body'.
contribution: It preserves the exact PATH location of the Company CLI, the symlink resolution chain through the shim, and the clarification that the executable body is in-repo, which helps any fused system locate and understand the control surface.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain entry documenting `/home/tim/.local/bin/company`, a symlink pointing to `~/company/ops/company`, a thin shim that launches the Company control CLI at `ops/cli/app.py`.
intention_for_embedding: To record the PATH-resolved location and in-repo target of the Company control CLI so the symlink is not mistaken for an external copy.
